Star Wars: The Clone Wars (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $35,161,554Details
International Box Office $33,533,889
Worldwide Box Office $68,695,443
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $27,504,254
Total $27,504,254
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Star Wars: The Clone Wars follows the Republic’s clone army in their struggle against Count Dooku’s Separatist forces, and features fan-favorite characters like Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano, and Darth Maul.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,611,273 (41.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.41 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:51.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$8,500,000 (worldwide box office is 8.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,452 opening theaters/3,452 max. theaters, 4.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $58,569,938

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 15th, 2008 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
Video Release: November 11th, 2008 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for sci-fi action violence throughout, brief language and momentary smoking.
Running Time: 98 minutes
Franchise: Star Wars
Geographical setting: Outer Space
Plot point: Cloning, Good vs. Evil, Kidnap, War
Social setting: Space Opera
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lucasfilm
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Star Wars: The Clone Wars follows the Republic’s clone army in their struggle against Count Dooku’s Separatist forces, and features fan-favorite characters like Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano, and Darth Maul.
